content.problemStatement:
The chefs are making fruit salads today! There are kkk chefs in total, each making a different type of salad. However, the chefs have run out of fruit, so they sent mmm workers to pick fruit for them.

Some workers are fast, but some are really slow. Specifically, for each worker, we know two integers aaa and bbb, which mean that this worker can pick between aaa and bbb units of fruit today (inclusive). There are nnn groups of workers; all workers in each group have the same a,ba, ba,b.

The workers don't care about the number of units of fruit they pick, so they choose these numbers randomly. A worker with parameters aaa, bbb will choose an integer between aaa and bbb uniformly randomly and pick this number of units of fruit today.

If the number of units of fruit picked by all workers together at the end of the day is SSS, the chefs define the goodness of the day as F(S)=SkF(S)=S^kF(S)=Sk for some reason. They would like you to find the expected value of FFF. It can be proven that this expected value can be written as a fraction P/QP/QP/Q, where P≥0P \ge 0P≥0 and Q>0Q \gt 0Q>0 are coprime integers. You should calculate P⋅Q−1P\cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998244353998244353998244353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 denotes the modular inverse of QQQ modulo 998244353998244353998244353. (It is guaranteed that this inverse exists and is unique.)
constraints: the sum of all CCC is equal to mmm
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers kkk and mmm.
The second line contains a single integer nnn.
Each of the following nnn lines contains three space-separated integers AAA, BBB and CCC denoting a group of workers; there are CCC workers in this group, with a=Aa=Aa=A and b=Bb=Bb=B for each of them.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer - the value of P⋅Q−1P\cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are two workers. The first worker always picks 111 unit of fruit. The second worker can pick 111 or 222 units. If he picks 111 unit, S=2S=2S=2 and F=4F=4F=4. If he picks 222 units, S=3S=3S=3 and F=9F=9F=9. Each situation happens with probability 1/21/21/2, so the expected value is 4/2+9/2=13/24/2+9/2=13/24/2+9/2=13/2. This means P=13P=13P=13 and Q=2Q=2Q=2, so Q−1=499122177Q^{-1}=499122177Q−1=499122177.
